41. Furnish the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of every calendar year a list of all its on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;

44. Retrieve the records of the properties found to have no tags and facilitate the placement/attachment of such Property Tags/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-referencing with the AREs and proper identification of the properties; and

49. We recommended that the LDRRMO prepare monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of report and submit within the prescribed period required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

51. We recommended that management direct the LDRRM Officer and the MDRRMC monitor the implementation of the LDRRM PPAs to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund within the targeted timelines.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that the MT  deposits the collections regularly and intact to avoid misuse of government funds, pursuant to Section 32, Volume 1 of MNGAS-LGU; and
Management sends demand letter to the previous MT for her undeposited collections and other cash accountabilities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the necessary adjusting journal entries to accurately reflect the correct balance of the CLT account in the books, thereby ensuring a fair and reliable presentation in the financial statements; and
Require the previous MT to process her claim for terminal leave benefits and to render an accounting of all her cash and accountabilities.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer promptly notify and provide the Municipal Accounting Office with the necessary documents requiring adjusting journal entries to ensure timely reflection in the financial reports;
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile regularly their respective cash records together with the records of the depository banks pertaining to the cash accounts of the agency to detect instantly errors or fraud either by the bank, Accounting unit or Cash unit; and
The Municipal Accountant draw a JEV to record the reconciling items upon receipt of the necessary supporting documents.

We recommended to management  require the GSO/Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to be guided by COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of physical count of PPE, preparation of necessary documents, reconciliation procedures, and recording in the books for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended and  Management agreed, to ensure the inventory committee prepares and submits the approved PIP to the Office of the Auditor at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory count; and

We recommended to management to require the Municipal Accountant effect the prompt transfer of the cost of completed projects from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books and depreciation expenses provided in the books in accordance with IPSAS.

We recommended the management to determine the status of settlement or collectability of these accounts, exhaust all available means to collect the same and consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant exert efforts to identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the FS. Further, Management to effect settlement of these reciprocal accounts to reflect an accurate financial condition for each fund.

We recommended the following to management:
Strictly observe the submission of required documents within the prescribed reglementary period for all future procurements for the immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, prompt detection or correction of deficiencies, and other necessary audit procedures;
Refrain from entering into contract with any winning bidder when all documentary requirements are not complied with; and
Orient all personnel, particularly those given the task to process procurement documents, to abide with the existing guidelines provided by the law and polices issued or adopted by the LGU in order to ensure prompt processing of documents without compromising the requirements of relevant law.

We recommended that management, on regular basis:
Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year; and
Inform the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommended the GAD Focal Person the following:
Maximize the utilization of GAD Funds through the implementation of GAD-related PPAs in order to attain the objectives for which funds were provided; and
For those PPAs already implemented but still have unutilized amounts, consider utilizing those amounts to implement new GAD-related PPAs to ensure more benefits to be derived for the intended constituents.

We recommended to the LDRRM Officer to prepare monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in its prescribed format as duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within 15 days after the end of each month.

We recommended that Collecting Officers delegated with the task of collecting taxes and other revenues of the LGU, be covered by a fidelity bond with the Bureau of Treasury and a bond sufficient to cover their cash accountability.

We recommended the following to the LSB:
Submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, within the timeline; and
Ensure the posting of the SEF Utilization Reports at the website of the LGU, if applicable, and/or in at least three conspicuous public places and the same be made not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2024-021.

4. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to recognize COS of items sold during the year and to properly reflect the carrying amount of Merchandise Inventory as of December 31, 2023; and

18. We recommended that the management refrain from splitting the procurement and consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at or around the same time by the same or different requisitioners. This will ensure compliance with the procurement process and prevent potential irregularities in the acquisition of goods and services.

19. The Municipal Budget Officer and implementing offices ensure that contracts awarded are within the approved budget and that obligations for payments do not exceed available appropriations;

20. The Municipal Development Council (MDC) ensure that proposed programs/projects are feasible and implementation ready; and

21. MDC adopt a system that will ensure its close monitoring of the execution of all PPAs to warrant their efficient and timely completion in order to deliver the desirable socio-economic and environmental development due to the public.

22. We recommended that management practice the conduct of public bidding or the most appropriate alternative mode of procurement. Should it consider availing the Direct Retail POL Products under Section 53.14 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, ensure that the necessary BAC Resolutions and Delegations are duly approved and that no preference over a certain supplier is observed to acquire the most reasonable prices. 23. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team Leader the check and the cash advance voucher together with the supporting documents with covering transmittal letter in a sealed envelope within seven days after release of check as required in the Joint Circular.

We recommended the following to management:

24. The LDRRM Officer prepare the Disbursement Voucher and the necessary documents in order to facilitate the return of the LDRRMO-CO to the General Fund totaling P4.566 million which was erroneously transferred to the Special Trust Fund Account;

25. The Municipal Budget Officer, correct and maintain the necessary registries for the LDRRM-CO continuing appropriations; and

26. The Municipal Accountant make the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balance for the Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management account. Only the Quick Respond Fund (QRF) and LDRRMF-MOOE unexpended/unobligated balances be transferred to the TF while The LDRRMF for CO is to be made continuing in the General Fund until the projects funded therefore are completed.

We recommended the following to management:

27. The LDRRM Officer prioritize the utilization of the LDRRMF prior years’ unexpended balance within the 5-year utilization period and ensure that such fund contributes in building a more disaster-resilient community; and

28. The Municipal Accountant prepare accounting reports for the unexpended balance of the DRRMF which exceeded the five-year period and facilitate the necessary transfer of funds to the unappropriated surplus of the GF. This amount is available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

29. 30. The Office of the Local Chief Executive facilitate the reimbursement of LDRRMF for the total amount spent for ineligible expenditures from the GF Proper; and

31. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and LDRRM Officer strictly follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF.

33. Inform the respective contractors of the structural defects and require them to correct the construction deficiencies at no cost to the Municipality; and

The Municipal Accountant to set up liabilities in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria set out in PPSASs.

We recommended the following to management:

The Municipal Budget Officer refrain from obligating subsequent year expenses against the current year appropriations;

The Municipal Accountant make the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the liability and government equity accounts and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments; and

The Municipal Accountant follow the accrual method of accounting Expense and Liability as prescribed in the Section 04 (a) and (s) of NGAS for LGUs, Vol. I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough analysis of the account and make necessary adjustments, as applicable, to rectify any discrepancies and ensure the proper valuation of the account. This will help to ensure the integrity of financial records and promote transparency and accountability in the financial management of the LGU.

We recommended that management:

Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of medicines and supplies in the appropriate inventory account/s, and recognize the corresponding expenses for actual issuances to the units; and

Require the conduct of physical count of inventories semestrally and accomplish the required RPCI to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the payable accounts and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments.

We recommended that management ensure that brand names, or if no brand is available state “no brand”, and complete detailed item specifications of the items procured are indicated in the Purchase Orders, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended to the management the following:

The BAC post in the PhilGEPS website the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed for the procurements done thru public bidding and all instances of alternative methods of procurement, except for contracts with ABC of Fifty Thousand Pesos (P50,000.00) and below; and

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the printout copy of postings of the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website is included among the supporting documents for payment of procured goods and services.

We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the mandated timelines in the submission of the monthly Trial Balance, Quarterly FS, Year-end FS and the DVs and the supporting documents pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the required SLs to substantiate the account balances reported in the 2022 Financial Statements. Henceforth, exert efforts to maintain the required books of accounts including subsidiary ledgers to comply with provisions of with the NGAS Manual for LGUs and PD No. 1445.

We recommended the following to management:

The OIC-HRMO ensure that all payments for salaries and other allowances of all personnel of the Municipality be strictly and properly supported with the DTRs, duly verified and signed by the employee’s immediate supervisor, Approved Application for Leave, Travel Order and Certificate of Appearance and other proofs of services rendered; and

The Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the completeness of supporting documents and/or requirements for every transaction to ensure compliance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LGU’s accounting unit reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/corrections, as necessary, in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant create and maintain a special account in the general fund for the receipt and utilization of the BGCM with complete and separate subsidiary ledgers, trial balance and financial statements; and

Post or disclose the Monthly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity Implementation in three conspicuous places such as Municipal Hall where the Bulletin Board is located and send a written notice to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, and Senate Committee on Finance, as required in LBC No. 125.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning Officer, BAC Chair, Municipal Accountant and/or other concerned Officials in custody of said contracts submit the same to the Auditor as prescribed and provide a written explanation for the delay in the submission.

We recommended the following:

The BAC post in the PhilGEPS website the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed for the procurements done thru public bidding and all instances of alternative methods of procurement, except for contracts with ABC of Fifty Thousand Pesos (P50,000.00) and below; and

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the printout copy of postings of the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website is included among the supporting documents for payment of procured goods and services.

We recommended that management:

Secure the applicable NTC licenses/permits to purchase, possess and operate the communication equipment prior to acquisition; and

Refrain from purchasing radio communication equipment from suppliers who do not have a valid NTC sellers’ permit.
We recommended and management agreed that the commendable practices shown by the collectors be continued.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly the BRS and submit the same within the reglementary period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 together with its supporting documents such as Monthly Bank Statement Snapshot to facilitate the early submission of the BRS, Subsidiary Ledgers, paid checks, debit and credit memos and deposit slips and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items noted in the process.

We recommended and management agreed that the LGU’s accounting unit reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/corrections, as necessary, in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.

We recommended that management:
Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of welfare goods, medical supplies and other goods in the appropriate inventory account/s, and recognize the corresponding expenses for actual issuances to the units; and
Submit the list of personnel involved in the receipt, custody and distribution of the welfare goods, and the original duly filled and signed Distribution List to determine the validity and regularity of DV no. 100-2021-09-2460 dated September 11, 2021.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to recognize COS of items sold during the year and to properly reflect the carrying amount of Merchandise Inventory as of December 31, 2021; and
Require the conduct of physical count of inventories semestrally and accomplish the required RPCI to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended and management agreed to require the following:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their records in order to determine the exact unexpended balance of the LDRRMF; and
The Municipal Accountant make the necessary journal entries to reflect correct balance for the payable account. Only the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE unexpended/unobligated balances shall be transferred to the Trust Fund while the LDRRMF for CO is to be made continuing in the General Fund until the projects funded therefore are completed.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accounting Office to make the necessary adjusting entries in order to reflect correct classifications of accounts and balances in the books.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that the Accountable Officers concerned immediately renew their bond sufficient to cover their actual accountabilities required under Treasury Circular No. 02-009 to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer advise the accountable officers to refrain from sharing their accountable forms with other collectors. Each accountable officer is responsible for the sole custody and issuance of accountable forms issued to him/her.

We recommended and management agreed that accountable officers properly and correctly record all collections made in their Cashbooks or equivalent records for easy verification, monitoring and reconciliation of cash balances with accounting and related records.

We recommended and management agreed to require the collecting officer to remit collections intact to the Municipal Treasurer on a daily basis. The Municipal Treasurer shall exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in overseeing accountable officers under her supervision, otherwise she shall be jointly and solidarily liable with them for the loss of government funds or property under their control.

We recommended and management agreed to maintain adequate internal control on cash by providing the concerned accountable officers a safe or vault where to keep undeposited collections, cash book and unused accountable forms. Moreover, provide a guard/ or watchman to secure the office during and after office hours, and likewise during the transport of the depositing accountable officer to the bank. Further, devise some measures to secure the physical structure of the office to provide protection against various risks.

We recommended and management agreed to adopt the use of ATM payroll system for all of its employees and require them to open an ATM payroll account with Land Bank of the Philippines to facilitate the crediting of salaries, wages and allowances to their account.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Direct the BAC to disqualify prospective suppliers whose authorized line of business is outside the nature of what is being procured;
Refrain from awarding contracts to those who are not technically, legally and financially capable as prescribed; and
Submit immediately the program/activity design on the disbursement voucher no. 100-2021-09-2461 dated September 2, 2021.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Submit valid justification why provision for OCM were used to pay for hauling services which should have been included as part of the Direct Costs if there is really a need for such; and
Strictly adhere with the guidelines on the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) as prescribed in DPWH D.O. No. 197, Series of 2016.

19.6 We recommended and management agreed to require:
19.6.1 Officials involved in the processing of claims review thoroughly the completeness of supporting documents and/or requirements for every transaction to ensure compliance with existing rules and regulations; and
19.6.2 Management refrain from paying the claims if these are not supported with the required documents and information to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the retention money from every progress payment or require the contractors to place standby letters of credit, bank guarantees and/or surety bonds before progress payments are made in strict adherence to Annex E, Paragraph 6 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended and management agreed to require:
The Sangguniang Bayan, thru the SB Secretary, submit to the Auditor’s Office copies of municipal ordinances establishing the economic enterprises of the LGU. If there is none, SB pass municipal ordinances for their creation in strict accordance with LBC No. 111;
The Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the quarterly, semi-annual and year-end financial statements for each LEE for evaluation together with the corresponding trial balances, BRS, and other relevant reports/schedules; and
Open individual bank accounts for each LEE where its own cash shall be maintained.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Immediately submit the bidding documents for the DVs identified in Appendix F; and
Refrain from paying the claims for delivered goods, services and infrastructure projects procured thru public bidding which are not supported with the required bidding documents to avoid suspensions in audit.

We recommended and management agreed to seek the required prior approval of the Sangguniang Bayan in relation to the implementation of projects charged against lump sum appropriations which are described in generic terms otherwise the projects/programs/activities in the AIP must be described with particularity to facilitate implementation.

We recommended and management agreed to enter into MOA with each of the beneficiary barangays regarding the implementation of development projects funded out of the Counterpart Fund. observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 1.)  refrain  from  granting  additional
cash  advances  to  Accountable  Officer  with  previous  unliquidated  cash
advances; 2.)  prepare supporting  schedule  of  Advances  to  Officers  and
Employees account to identify the accountable officers concerned  and  Aging
of Cash Advances Schedule; 3.)  enforce full liquidation  of  cash  advances
within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular  No.97-002  dated
February 10, 1997; and 4.)  demand the  concerned  officials  and  employees
with unliquidated cash advances  to  effect  immediate  settlement  of  cash
advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement  as  provided
for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated  November  28,  2012
shall be enforced.

We recommend the following:  1.)  replenishment of the  PCF   be  made
when the disbursement reaches at least 75 percent or as the need  arises  of
the cash advance; and 2.)  full liquidation or return of the unutilized  PCF
at the end of each year for closing. Also, the  same  be  done  in  case  of
termination, resignation, retirement or dismissal or when  the  purpose  for
setting up the fund has already been served.

We recommend the following, to wit:  1.) the Municipal  Accountant  to
book up as Expenses all obligations incurred during the year as required  in
Section 119 of P.D. 1445; 2.) at the end of the  year  and  in  cases  where
payment cannot be effected yet, said  obligations  shall  be  booked  up  as
Expense and the corresponding Accounts Payable be set up  at  the  year  the
expenses were incurred;  and 3.)  the Municipal  Budget  Officer  to  adopt
appropriate measures to control the charges made  under  the  appropriations
of programs, projects and activities to which it was created and  to  ensure
that all obligations  are  duly  backed  up  with  cash  and  not  only  the
availability of appropriations but the cash availability as well.

We recommend the following, to wit:  1.) the Municipal Budget  Officer
to adopt  appropriate  measures  to  control  the  charges  made  under  the
appropriations of programs, projects and activities to which it was  created
and to ensure that all obligations are duly backed  up  with  cash  and  not
only the availability of appropriations but the cash availability  as  well;
2.) include during the budget preparation  the  provision  of  extraordinary
and miscellaneous expenses/discretionary fund in the budget; and 3.)  follow
strictly the limitation in appropriating discretionary fund for  the  office
of the local chief executive as provided for under  Section  325(h)  of  R.A
No. 7160.

We recommend that:  1.) officials involved in the processing of claims
review  thoroughly  the  completeness   of   supporting   documents   and/or
requirements for every transaction to ensure compliance with existing  rules
and regulations; and 2.) management refrain from paying the claims if  these
are not supported with the  required  documents  and  information  to  avoid
suspending the transaction in audit.

We recommend that management strictly comply with  the  provisions  of
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to facilitate  the  review
and evaluation process and attention is also invited to the  penalty  clause
provision of said Circular.

We recommend  that:   1.)   The  Municipal  Planning  and  Development
Coordinator  and  the  Municipal  Development   Council   to   monitor   the
implementation of the projects as  incorporated  in  the  Annual  Investment
Plan; 2.)   The  Municipal  Engineer  to  coordinate  with  the  departments
concerned to fast track the implementation of the  projects;  and  3.)   The
Local  Chief  Executive  to  ensure  compliance  with  the  responsibilities
provided for in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that:  1.) the  Supply  Officer  Designate  or  Personnel
assigned as in charge in the control of the usage of  the  vehicles  of  the
municipality ensure that every liquidation and utilization of fuel, oil  and
lubricants be supported by Driver’s Ticket  with complete information  other
than the requisition to monitor the usage thereof; and 2.)  to  prepare  and
submit to the Auditor’s  Office  Monthly  Report  of  Fuel  Consumption  and
Report of Official Travels for verification.

We recommend that the salaries/wages of  casual/contractual  employees
under the Contracts of Service and Job Order Appointment must  be  submitted
to the Civil Service Commission,  Provincial  Office  for  review  and  also
submit the necessary supporting documents in the  payment  of  salaries  and
wages as provided  in  COA  Circular  No.  2012-001  dated  June  14,  2012,
Prescribing the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements  for  Common
Government Transactions.

It is recommended that management 1.) refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officer with previous unliquidated cash advances; 2.)  prepare supporting schedule of Advances to Officers and Employees account to identify the accountable officers concerned and Aging of Cash Advances Schedule; 3.)  enforce full liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No.97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and 4.)  demand the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement of cash advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 shall be enforced.
observations.reason:
Observation Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of CY 2016 AAR

Demand letters were sent to employees that that outstanding cash advances until the present period.  As a result, some of the employees have already liquidated their cash advance.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Accountant determine the nature and intention of the fund transfers before preparing the entries to account for the transfers made.  In addition, a separate set of books for the economic enterprise shall be maintained.

It is  recommended that the 1)  Municipal Accountant evaluate the claims for hazard pay allowance and subsistence allowance of the PHWs in conformity with the provisions of DBM and DOH Joint Circular No. 1, S. 2012; and  2)  concerned PHWs  to refund the excess amount of hazard pay allowance and subsistence allowance claimed.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant, examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction before payment shall be done to avoid possible suspensions and/or disallowances in audit and to strictly comply with the provisions set in DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.

It is recommended that management should require the BAC to evaluate the frequency of Direct Contracting being resorted to and to ensure that the conditions for Direct Contracting as an alternative mode of procurement have been satisfied and practice the conduct of public bidding in the procurement process in order to ensure the most advantageous price and generate income from the sale of bid documents.

It is recommended that management impose warranty security to be posted by the contractors on completed infrastructure projects after the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance following the prescribed schedule in Section 62.2.3.3. of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

It is recommended that management reconcile the unexpended LDRRMF balance as reported in the Trust Fund books and in the Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund Utilization Report and transfer the correct balance to the Trust Fund bank account.

We recommend that management: 1.) adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the grant of financial assistance to NGOs/POs and ensure that the supporting documents are complete and attached before donations to such groups are approved; 2.) create a committee to screen and evaluate the qualifications of the NGOs/POs and their advocacies/commitments vis-à-vis the provisions of the cited regulations and following a rational financial assistance plan; 3.) make the following correcting entry and to strictly observe proper classification of accounts in all its transactions to be able to present a reliable Financial Statements: Debit to Due from Non-Government Organizations/ People's Organizations in the amount of  250,000.00 and credit to Donations in the amount of 250,000.00, respectively; and    4.) Send letters to the NGO/PO demanding liquidation of their outstanding financial assistance pursuant to Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.
